content.sentence: Máy tính này có ___ rất nhanh.

translation.en: This computer has a fast circuit.

example.es: El vaso sanguíneo en el cuerpo es muy importante.
example.en: The blood vessel in the body is very important.
example.vi: Mạch máu trong cơ thể rất quan trọng.

definitions.en: A blood vessel or a channel through which blood flows.
definitions.vi: Một mạch máu hoặc một kênh mà qua đó máu chảy.
definitions.es: Un vaso sanguíneo o un canal por el que fluye la sangre.

notes_md:
**mạch** usually means **pulse** — the beat you feel from someone’s heart, especially at the wrist or neck. It’s a noun; you often say *bắt mạch* (check someone’s pulse) or *đo mạch* (measure pulse).

It also very commonly means a **vein** or **blood vessel** in the body (*mạch máu*). More broadly, *mạch* can mean a **line/flow** of something, like electricity (*mạch điện*) or water (*mạch nước*).

A super common phrase is *một mạch*, meaning “in one go” or “without stopping,” like doing something continuously — think of it as one unbroken flow.
exampleSentences: Bác sĩ kiểm tra mạch của chị khi chị đến khám bệnh.
Anh cảm thấy mạch của mình đập nhanh khi chạy bộ buổi sáng.
Em học cách đo mạch để chăm sóc người thân tốt hơn.
notes:
mạch usually means pulse — the beat you feel from someone’s heart, especially at the wrist or neck. It’s a noun; you often say bắt mạch (check someone’s pulse) or đo mạch (measure pulse).

It also very commonly means a vein or blood vessel in the body (mạch máu). More broadly, mạch can mean a line/flow of something, like electricity (mạch điện) or water (mạch nước).

A super common phrase is một mạch, meaning “in one go” or “without stopping,” like doing something continuously — think of it as one unbroken flow.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The doctor checks your pulse when you come for a checkup.
I feel my pulse beat fast when I jog in the morning.
I learn how to measure pulse to take better care of my family.

translation.en: This computer has a fast circuit.

content.sentence: Máy tính này có ___ rất nhanh.
